preview_text:
The model favors Belgium with a 64% win probability, establishing them as clear favorites on a neutral pitch. New Zealand's chances of victory stand at 11%, roughly one-sixth of Belgium's likelihood.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome in the model's forecast at 24%, suggesting the match has room for a competitive, closely contested result. The 40-percentage-point gap between Belgium's win probability and New Zealand's reflects genuine separation in the matchup, yet the elevated draw probability indicates neither team is heavily favored to dominate decisively.

The market has shifted notably more in Belgium's favor than the model, pricing their win at 77% compared to the model's 64%. The sportsbook consensus assigns New Zealand only 8% and a draw 15%, gaps of roughly 3 and 9 percentage points respectively below the model. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with sportsbook sentiment. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct, only that meaningful divergence exists across these sources.
